specs.title: Customer Identity Validation
specs.narrative: Before any claim intake details are captured, the chatbot must verify that the customer is the policyholder of record. Identity validation checks the policy number and the customer's date of birth against the Customer Master record. On success, the conversation continues to claim detail capture; on failure, the customer is offered a single retry with a clear, non-leaky error message.

screens.purpose: Collect policy number + DOB so the system can validate that the chatbot user is the policyholder before any claim data is captured.
screens.layout:
- Single-column conversational layout (web + mobile)
- Header: chatbot avatar + step indicator (1 of 4)
- Body: two prompts in sequence (policy number, then DOB) with inline confirm
- Accessible: WCAG 2.1 AA contrast; full keyboard navigation; screen-reader announces each prompt
screens.navigation:
- 'Back' returns to chatbot welcome
- 'Cancel' aborts the claim session (with confirm dialog)
- On successful validation: forward to Claim Intake Form
- On failed validation (after 1 retry): forward to Customer Support Handoff
screens.interactive_behavior:
- Initial: policy-number prompt focused with placeholder 'e.g. POL-1234567'
- Loading: spinner + 'Verifying…' (max 3s; longer triggers a timeout banner)
- On error: inline message 'We couldn't verify your details. Please try again.' (no PII echoed)
- Session expiry after 15 minutes idle → redirect with 'session expired' banner

definition.tooltip: 10–12 character policy ID from your policy document

validation.message: Policy number format is invalid. It should look like POL-1234567.

definition.tooltip: Date of birth as registered on your policy

validation.message: We couldn't verify your details. Please try again.

request_spec.validation: both fields mandatory; policy_id format; dob within last 150 years

fields.customer_token: opaque 64-char session token; only on success

error_handling.fallback: Customer Support Handoff on 429 or 503 after 2 retries

security.masking: log policy_id last-4 only; never log dob
security.audit: audit row per attempt with outcome + ip + ua

operational.rate_limit: 5 attempts per session per 15 minutes; 100/min per IP
operational.bc_rules: v1 must remain available for 12 months after v2 launch

data_entities.description: Append-only log of every identity validation attempt — used for rate limiting + audit.
data_entities.access_logic: Append-only writes per attempt. Reads scoped to a single session_id for rate-limit check. No joins to Customer Master — that lookup goes through the upstream service.
data_entities.retention_policy: 18 months; purged via daily batch job

business_rules.description: Maximum 5 validation attempts per session per 15-minute window.
Identity error messages must be non-leaky (same message for invalid policy as for DOB mismatch).
business_rules.logic_decision: on any 401 / business failure → identical message 'We couldn't verify your details.'

acceptance_scenarios.given: a valid customer with policy POL-1234567 and DOB 1985-06-12
acceptance_scenarios.when: they submit identity verification via the chatbot
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns ok=true with a session token and routes them to the Claim Intake Form

acceptance_scenarios.given: a customer enters policy POL-1234567 with the wrong DOB
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 401 with message 'We couldn't verify your details.' and shows a single retry prompt

acceptance_scenarios.given: a customer with no policy on file
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 401 with the same non-leaky message and offers the support handoff after retry exhaustion

acceptance_scenarios.given: a customer who has already attempted validation 5 times within 15 minutes
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 429 TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS and routes them to Customer Support Handoff

acceptance_scenarios.given: the upstream Customer Master is unavailable
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 503 UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE, retries with exponential backoff, and offers the support handoff after 2 retries
policy_id is stored as last-4 only and the dob is never persisted

functional_requirements.requirement_text: The system must validate the policy_id + date_of_birth pair against the Customer Master and return a session token on match.
The system must return a generic, non-leaky 401 message on any validation failure, regardless of whether the policy or the DOB is the root cause.
The system must enforce a rate limit of 5 attempts per session per 15-minute window and return 429 on the 6th attempt.
On upstream unavailability, the system must retry with exponential backoff and degrade gracefully to a Customer Support Handoff after 2 retries.
Identity attempts must be audited with policy_id last-4 only; the date of birth must never be persisted.

spec_decisions.question: Should the chatbot use a session-bound rate limit (5/session) or an IP-based limit (100/IP/min)?

options.label: Session-bound
options.description: Track attempts per chatbot session in Redis with a 15-min TTL.
options.implications: Tighter UX; bypass possible by starting a fresh session.

options.label: IP-based
options.description: Track attempts per IP across sessions.
options.implications: Harder to bypass; can affect shared-IP corporate customers.

spec_decisions.recommended_rationale: Session-bound is friendlier for legitimate retries and matches the existing claims chatbot session model in the App Brain.

traceability.target_label: Customer identity must be verified before any claim intake.
Customer identity must be verified before any claim intake.
Customer identity must be verified before any claim intake.

specs.independent_test: From a fresh chatbot session, attempt validation 6 times against a known policy with the wrong DOB. Observe: every attempt 1–5 returns the same non-leaky 401 message; attempt 6 returns 429 and routes to support. Then start a new session and attempt validation against the correct policy/DOB; observe a 200 OK with a session token.
specs.data_and_validation: Inputs: policy_id (10–12 alphanumeric with hyphen) and date_of_birth (ISO 8601). Both mandatory. Server-side validation enforces format AND consistency against Customer Master. No PII echoed on failure.
specs.errors_and_edge_cases: 400 on malformed input. 401 on validation failure (generic message). 429 on rate-limit exhaustion. 503 with retry on upstream unavailability. After 2 retries on 503 or any 429, the user is routed to Customer Support Handoff with a session context handle so the agent has the attempt log.
specs.observability: Per-attempt audit row (entity above). Counter metric: claim_intake.validation.{outcome}. Distribution metric: claim_intake.validation.latency_ms. Trace correlation id propagated to Customer Master call.

implementation_tasks.task: Add Redis-backed rate limiter for session
Verify PII redaction in audit log

specs.title: Claim Detail Capture Form
specs.narrative: After identity verification, the chatbot guides the customer through capturing core claim details (incident date, claim type, brief description, supporting documents). §1 UI Specification omitted pending Figma link. Backend + data + scenarios are designed against the conceptual UI defined by the BRD's stakeholder needs.

request_spec.validation: incident_date within 6 months past + not future; claim_type allowed_values; description trimmed

error_handling.retry: client retries with same Idempotency-Key for transient 503
error_handling.fallback: save draft for later if 503 persists

security.audit: audit row per submission with claim_id

data_entities.description: Captured claim details awaiting downstream Claims Triage and adjudication.
data_entities.access_logic: Insert-only from this FRS. Reads scoped by claim_id (single-row) or by customer_token_hash for history. Triage service reads separately.
data_entities.retention_policy: Retained for 7 years per claims compliance policy

business_rules.description: Incident date must be within the last 6 months and not in the future.
business_rules.logic_decision: if incident_date > today or incident_date < today - 6 months then return 400 INVALID_DETAIL

business_rules.description: Duplicate claims (same customer + incident_date + claim_type within 24 hours) must not be created.
business_rules.logic_decision: if matching customer_token_hash + incident_date + claim_type exists within 24h then return 409 DUPLICATE_SUBMISSION

acceptance_scenarios.given: an authenticated customer with a valid session token
acceptance_scenarios.when: they submit a claim detail with incident_date 2 weeks ago and claim_type 'motor'
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 201 with a CLM- prefixed claim_id and status 'captured'

acceptance_scenarios.when: they submit a claim with incident_date 8 months ago
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 400 INVALID_DETAIL with a message pointing at the date field

acceptance_scenarios.given: an authenticated customer who has just submitted a motor claim for 2026-05-15
acceptance_scenarios.when: they retry the same submission within 24 hours
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 409 DUPLICATE_SUBMISSION with the original claim_id

acceptance_scenarios.given: a customer whose session has expired
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 401 SESSION_EXPIRED and redirects to identity verification

acceptance_scenarios.given: the same Idempotency-Key is used twice within 24h
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns the original 201 response without re-creating the claim

acceptance_scenarios.given: the upstream document store is unavailable
acceptance_scenarios.when: the customer submits a claim referencing UPL-12345
acceptance_scenarios.then: the system returns 503 UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE and the client retries with the same Idempotency-Key

functional_requirements.requirement_text: The system must capture and persist claim_type, incident_date, description, and document_refs and return a CLM- prefixed claim_id.
Incident date must be within the past 6 months and not in the future; otherwise return 400.
Duplicate submissions (same customer + incident_date + claim_type) within 24 hours must return 409 with the original claim_id.
Expired sessions must return 401 SESSION_EXPIRED and trigger the identity-verification flow.
Requests with a repeated Idempotency-Key within 24 hours must return the original response without side-effects.
On 503 upstream errors, the system must permit safe client retry using the same Idempotency-Key.

traceability.target_label: Customer can submit a new claim through the chatbot.
Customer can submit a new claim through the chatbot.

specs.independent_test: Via the chatbot, complete identity verification, then submit a claim detail with valid fields. Observe a 201 with a CLM- claim_id. Repeat the same submission immediately — observe 409 with the same claim_id returned. Then submit with an Idempotency-Key already used in the 24h window — observe the original 201 response.
specs.data_and_validation: Inputs: incident_date (mandatory, within 6 months past, not future), claim_type (enum), description (≤2000 chars, trimmed), document_refs (≤10 IDs from the upload service). Idempotency-Key required.
specs.errors_and_edge_cases: 400 for date out of window or claim_type not allowed. 401 for expired session. 409 for duplicate. 503 for upstream unavailability — client may retry safely with the same Idempotency-Key.
specs.observability: Audit row per submission. Counter: claim_intake.submission.{status}. Distribution: claim_intake.submission.latency_ms. Trace propagation to downstream Claims Triage.

specs.title: Claim Triage Handoff
specs.narrative: Once a claim_draft is created, it must be handed off to the Claims Triage module for severity classification and operator assignment. The handoff is an asynchronous event with at-least-once delivery and an outbox pattern. Error envelope and audit format follow the Cross-cutting Standards FRS (M000-FRS001).

response_spec.structure: Outbox row write only; no synchronous body

error_handling.retry: outbox-relay retries every 30s up to 24h; then dead-letter

security.auth_method: mTLS between Claim Intake and event bus
security.audit: outbox row + relay row form the audit trail

operational.transaction_boundary: claim_draft INSERT + outbox INSERT in one tx

business_rules.description: The claim_submitted event must be persisted in the outbox in the same transaction as the claim_draft row.
business_rules.applies_to: Submit Claim Detail endpoint + Publish event
business_rules.logic_decision: BEGIN; INSERT claim_draft; INSERT outbox; COMMIT — if either fails, no event is emitted

business_rules.description: Error envelope for any operational error on this handoff follows M000-FRS001 (Cross-cutting Standards).
business_rules.applies_to: All emitted errors on this FRS
business_rules.logic_decision: use {code, message, retryable, correlation_id} per M000-FRS001

acceptance_scenarios.when: the outbox row is written in the same transaction
acceptance_scenarios.then: the Claims Triage module observes a claim_submitted event with the matching claim_id within 30 seconds
no outbox row exists and no event is emitted (atomicity)

acceptance_scenarios.given: the outbox-relay worker crashes after reading a row but before publishing
acceptance_scenarios.then: the unprocessed outbox row is republished and the Triage module dedups by claim_id (at-least-once semantics)

acceptance_scenarios.given: the event bus is unavailable for 30 minutes
acceptance_scenarios.then: rows are retried with exponential backoff and alarms fire after 1 hour of failures

acceptance_scenarios.given: an outbox row that has been retried for 24 hours
acceptance_scenarios.when: it has still not been published successfully
acceptance_scenarios.then: the row is moved to dead-letter and an operator alert is raised with the correlation_id
the payload conforms to the v1 schema in this FRS and any extra fields are ignored

functional_requirements.requirement_text: On successful claim_draft creation, the system must publish a claim_submitted event with claim_id, customer_segment, claim_type, and incident_date.
The claim_draft INSERT and outbox INSERT must happen in a single transaction; failure of either prevents both.
The outbox-relay must implement at-least-once delivery; consumers must dedup by claim_id.
Failed publish attempts must be retried with exponential backoff for up to 24 hours; after that, the row is dead-lettered and an operator alert is raised.
The event payload must conform to the v1 schema; consumers must ignore unknown fields (forward compatibility).

traceability.target_label: Common error response envelope for backend services

specs.independent_test: Submit a valid claim via M001-FRS002 and observe the Claims Triage queue for a claim_submitted event with the matching claim_id arriving within 30 seconds. Then disconnect the database mid-submission and verify that neither a claim_draft row nor an outbox row exists. Finally, take the event bus offline for 5 minutes and observe that outbox-relay retries until the bus is restored, with no events lost.
specs.data_and_validation: Inputs come from the claim_draft INSERT (same transaction). claim_id format CLM-XXXXXXXXXX (10 alphanumeric). Outbox payload size limited to 8KB.
specs.errors_and_edge_cases: Transactional rollback on either INSERT failing. Outbox-relay retries with exponential backoff. Dead-lettering after 24h. Consumer-side dedup by claim_id. All error envelopes follow M000-FRS001.
specs.observability: Outbox depth gauge. claim_intake.handoff.published counter. claim_intake.handoff.dead_letter counter. Per-event tracing with correlation_id.

open_questions.question: Should the rate-limit window for identity validation be tunable per business unit?
open_questions.why: Operational teams may want to relax the limit for corporate brokers managing many clients.
open_questions.example: 5 per session for retail; 20 per session for corporate broker accounts
